what_is_genuinely_good: prefers-reduced-motion is handled comprehensively across both CSS files — almost every animation has a matching reduce override. Rare and appreciated.

ARIA is unusually thorough: skip link, tablist with arrow-key nav, progressbar role with aria-valuenow/min/max, aria-live on sync banner, role=alert on error banners, sr-only labels on decorative elements. This is clearly intentional and well-executed.
Polling is smart: visibility-aware interval switching (30s active / 60s hidden), plus a 10-minute idle timeout that halts polls when the user is away. No unnecessary hammering.

The vibe system is cohesive end-to-end — the same vibe key drives avatar faces, whiteboard text, stats bar tint, grid line color, manager nameplate, conference table chairs, and speech bubble quotes. Impressive data-attribute-driven theming.

Custom SVG characters are delightful and well-labeled with aria-label. Each character has personality (Dave's headphones, Sofia's magnifying glass, Luna's moon). The bloberto face variants per vibe are excellent.
Light/dark theme is correctly implemented with CSS custom properties + data-theme attribute + localStorage persistence. The theme toggle aria-label correctly announces the target state, not current.
Worker click → tab switch → focused card scroll is a satisfying UX detail. The 3s auto-clear is appropriate.
stableActivityLog intent is smart: avoid re-rendering all WorkerCards on every log append by memoizing on length. The intent is right even if execution has a bug.

suggestions.title: Side effect inside useMemo breaks stableActivityLog
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 764-767: `const stableActivityLog = useMemo(() => { activityLogLenRef.current = activityLog.length; return activityLog }, [activityLog.length])`. Two problems: (1) Setting a ref inside useMemo is a React anti-pattern — useMemo is not guaranteed to run only once per dep change and can be discarded by concurrent React. (2) Memoizing on `activityLog.length` but returning `activityLog` means if entries are mutated in-place or replaced with the same count, the memo returns stale data silently. Fix: move the ref update to a separate `useEffect(() => { activityLogLenRef.current = activityLog.length }, [activityLog.length])` and memo the log on the array reference itself — or just pass `activityLog` directly if the optimization isn't measurably necessary.

suggestions.title: Mobile: whiteboard text clips to 'FL\nOW' at 375px
suggestions.description: Confirmed visually in the mobile screenshot: the whiteboard text '⚡ FLOW' wraps and clips mid-word, displaying as 'FL / OW' due to the `.office-whiteboard` having `overflow: hidden` combined with an insufficient width at small viewports. The `.wb-vibe-text` lacks `white-space: nowrap`. Fix: add `white-space: nowrap` to `.wb-vibe-text` in Office.css, and/or increase the whiteboard min-width in the `@media (max-width: 480px)` block. Cross-check all other vibe labels ('🚨 HELP', 'TODO: ???', '🙏 ZZZ') for similar wrapping.

suggestions.title: Mobile: shortcut toast overlaps footer, making both unreadable
suggestions.description: Confirmed in mobile screenshot: at 375px the shortcut toast (fixed, bottom: 1rem) renders directly over the footer, producing an overlapping text mess of 'Built with 💜 and mild existential dread / 1 Office · 2 Dashboard · R Refresh · Esc Clear / it. v1.0.0-chaos'. The toast `z-index: 9999` wins, partially covering the footer. Fix: add a `@media (max-width: 600px)` rule to `.shortcut-toast` that sets `bottom: 4rem` to clear the footer height, or render the toast above the tab bar instead of at the page bottom on mobile.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar milestone effect exits loop on first match, skipping later milestones
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 183-190: the `useEffect` loops over `[25, 50, 75, 100]`, and when a milestone is hit it calls `return () => clearTimeout(t)` — this `return` exits the for-loop immediately. If progress jumps from 0% to 80% in a single update, only the 25% milestone fires; 50% and 75% are skipped entirely. Fix: collect all newly-crossed milestones first, then trigger only the highest (or all in sequence with staggered timeouts): `const newMilestones = MILESTONES.filter(m => progress >= m && !milestonesRef.current.has(m)); for (const m of newMilestones) milestonesRef.current.add(m); if (newMilestones.length > 0) { setFlashClass(...); ... }`

suggestions.title: completionToast uses 20 lines of inline styles — breaks light mode theming
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 952-972: the completion toast is rendered with a full inline `style={{}}` block including hardcoded `borderRadius: '12px'`, `padding: '0.6rem 1.2rem'`, `fontSize: '0.95rem'`, `zIndex: 9999`, dynamic boxShadow with rgba glow, etc. This prevents CSS-variable overrides from `[data-theme='light']` from applying (the `background: 'var(--surface2)'` works, but the `boxShadow` with `rgba(0,0,0,0.4)` looks wrong in light mode). Extract a `.completion-toast` class to App.css with `--toast-color` as a CSS variable, then pass `style={{ '--toast-color': completionToast.color }}` only.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ROLE_COLORS in App.jsx duplicates and diverges from ROLE_COLORS in constants.js
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 14-20 define `WORKER_ROLE_COLORS` keyed by worker *name* (Maya, Carlos, Dave...) while constants.js has `ROLE_COLORS` keyed by *role*. The hex values differ: ROLE_COLORS has Maya as `#a78bfa` but WORKER_ROLE_COLORS has Maya as `#a855f7`. WORKER_ROLE_COLORS is only used for the completion confetti color (line 831). If a new team member is added, two separate files need updating with potentially inconsistent values. Fix: derive from `ROLE_COLORS` via the worker's role at the call site: `const color = ROLE_COLORS[worker.role] ?? '#a855f7'`. Delete WORKER_ROLE_COLORS.

suggestions.title: Tab title rotation resets every 30s because activeWorkers is a new array reference on every poll
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 888-910: the `useEffect` that cycles `document.title` every 8s depends on `[activeWorkers, teamVibe]`. Since `activeWorkers` is computed via `useMemo` from `allWorkers` (which is replaced via `setAllWorkers` on every successful fetch), a new array is produced every 30s. This clears and restarts the interval before it can cycle through all 3 frames, meaning the title always resets to frame 0 and effectively never rotates. Fix: extract stable primitives as deps: `[workingCount, idleCount, errCount, total, teamVibe.label]`.

suggestions.title: LoadingSkeleton is entirely inline-styled — extract to CSS classes
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 484-493: the three skeleton cards each contain multiple `style={{ width: 44, height: 44, borderRadius: 10 }}`, `style={{ width: '55%', height: 14 }}` etc. inline style objects. React allocates a new object per render per prop. These are static values that never change — they belong in CSS modifier classes like `.skeleton--avatar`, `.skeleton--title`, `.skeleton--subtitle`, `.skeleton--badge`. This also makes it trivially easy to update skeleton sizing across all cards.

suggestions.title: Ghost/non-interactive Character components get tabIndex={0} with no keyboard action
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 419: `tabIndex={0}` is applied unconditionally to all Character components. Ghost characters (OOO roster members) show an OOO bubble on hover but have no `onClick` and no keyboard equivalent. Keyboard users will tab onto these elements, focus them, press Enter/Space, and nothing happens — no feedback, no action. `role` is only set when `handleClick` is defined, leaving ghost chars as unfocusable divs pretending to be interactive. Fix: only set `tabIndex={0}` when `handleClick` is defined OR when the ghost bubble behavior is explicitly made keyboard-accessible with an onKeyDown handler.

suggestions.title: Coffee corner renders as oversized orange/gradient block on desktop
suggestions.description: Confirmed visually in the desktop screenshot: the coffee corner in the top-right of the office floor appears as a large solid orange-pink rectangle (roughly 60×80px) rather than a compact coffee cup icon. The surrounding 'CHATTING' and 'COFFEE' labels are visible but the block dominates. This appears to be `.coffee-corner__body` inheriting or computing an unexpectedly large size — possibly the `.coffee-corner[data-vibe='in-flow']` background color rule bleeding into the body container. Inspect and add an explicit max-width/max-height to `.coffee-corner__body` to constrain it.

suggestions.title: Tag filter has no accessible announcement when applied
suggestions.description:
When a task tag (Bug/QA/Design/etc.) is clicked and `selectedTag` is set, worker cards are dimmed/filtered silently. Screen reader users get no feedback that the view has changed. Fix: add an `aria-live='polite'` status element to the Dashboard panel that announces: 'Showing workers tagged [tag]' or 'Filter cleared'. This is a one-line addition: `
{selectedTag ? `Showing workers tagged ${selectedTag}` : 'All workers shown'}
`

suggestions.title: Activity log key construction risks collision — missing separator
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 130: `key={entry.timestamp + entry.worker + i}`. JavaScript coerces all operands to string then concatenates. `timestamp=1234, worker='', i=5` → '12345' and `timestamp=123, worker='4', i=5` → '12345' are identical keys. React will silently reuse the wrong DOM node. Fix: use a separator: `` key={`${entry.timestamp}::${entry.worker ?? ''}::${i}`} ``

suggestions.title: WallClock static transformOrigin applied as inline style — move to CSS
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 685 and 692: `style={{ transformOrigin: '11px 11px', transform: 'rotate(...)' }}`. The `transformOrigin` value is always `'11px 11px'` (the clock center) and never changes. Only the `transform` rotate value is dynamic. Fix: define `.office-wall-clock g { transform-origin: 11px 11px; }` in Office.css and only pass `style={{ transform: 'rotate(...)' }}` inline.

suggestions.title: vibeHistory read/write is split across App and StatsBar — hard to trace
suggestions.description: The vibe history is written to localStorage in App's useEffect (line 861) but read back from localStorage inside StatsBar's useEffect (line 400). This split ownership means the full data lifecycle spans two components with no shared abstraction. If the key or format changes, two separate effects must be updated. Fix: extract a `useVibeHistory(vibeKey)` custom hook that handles both read and write, returning the history array.

suggestions.title: Two overlapping confetti layers can fire simultaneously — 60 animated elements
suggestions.description: App.jsx renders both `confettiActive` (vibe rank increase, 30 pieces) and `workerConfetti` (task completion, 30 pieces) as separate layers. Both can be truthy simultaneously — a worker shipping a task while the team vibe improves will create 60 animated confetti elements at once. On low-end mobile this can cause jank. Fix: use a single confetti layer and merge the two triggers with a priority system, or limit total confetti count to 20 per layer with `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference)` gating.

suggestions.title: Decorative plant positions hardcoded as inline styles in JSX — use CSS or constants
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 985, 995, 1003, 1012: decorative plants use `style={{ left: '87%', top: '72%' }}` etc. directly in JSX. This makes layout adjustments require touching JSX structure instead of CSS. These are static positions that will never change at runtime. Fix: add `.office-plant--ficus`, `.office-plant--cactus`, and position variants as CSS rules, removing the inline style props entirely.

suggestions.title: Sync button wraps ↺ character without aria-hidden, possibly double-announced
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 996: ``. The ↺ (U+21BA) character is a Unicode symbol that some screen readers announce as 'anticlockwise open circle arrow'. Since `aria-label` overrides the inner text on the button element itself, this is technically fine — but it's safer to explicitly silence the symbol: `↺` inside the button. Same pattern applies to the retry buttons (↺ Retry) in error banners at lines 1012 and 1018 which do NOT have aria-label and will be read as '↺ Retry'.
